report_name: Actively Exploited Zero-Day Flaw (CVE-2026-0300) in PAN-OS Allows Root Access on Palo Alto Networks Firewalls

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: An unauthenticated remote attacker can exploit a buffer overflow vulnerability in the internet-facing Captive Portal feature of Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS to achieve arbitrary code execution with root privileges.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The vulnerability in the User-ID Authentication Portal, a remote service on PAN-OS firewalls, is actively exploited to gain root-level remote code execution.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The PAN-OS Captive Portal, an external remote service, was exploited by unauthenticated attackers to gain initial access and complete control of affected firewalls.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-0300
vulnerabilities.description: A critical buffer overflow vulnerability in the PAN-OS User-ID Authentication Portal (Captive Portal) allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code with root privileges.

indicators.name: Palo Alto Networks Captive Portal Access Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Palo Alto Networks Captive Portal Access Attempt
id: 2b3c1a9d-8e4f-4b1a-9c8d-7e6f5d4c3b2a
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects web requests to the default URL for the Palo Alto Networks Captive Portal. Inbound requests to this URL from the internet are a primary indicator of exposure to CVE-2026-0300.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/05/10/week-in-review-cpanel-vulnerability-actively-exploited-digicert-breach-linkedin-job-scams/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem|contains: '/php/login.php'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the Palo Alto Networks Captive Portal login page, which is the entry point for exploiting CVE-2026-0300.

indicators.name: Palo Alto Networks authd Process Crash
indicators.pattern:
title: Palo Alto Networks authd Process Crash
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-8b1a-9c8d7e6f5d4c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects logs indicating a crash or restart of the 'authd' process on Palo Alto Networks firewalls. This could indicate an exploitation attempt against CVE-2026-0300.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/05/10/week-in-review-cpanel-vulnerability-actively-exploited-digicert-breach-linkedin-job-scams/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: panos
  category: system
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'authd'
    - 'crash'
    - 'restarting'
  condition: all of them
indicators.description: Detects crashes or restarts of the 'authd' process on Palo Alto Networks firewalls, which could indicate an exploitation attempt against CVE-2026-0300.

indicators.name: Palo Alto Networks authd Core Dump File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Palo Alto Networks authd Core Dump File Creation
id: 5a4b3c2d-1e9f-4a8b-9c7d-6e5f4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the creation of core dump files for the 'authd' process, a potential indicator of successful or attempted exploitation of CVE-2026-0300.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/05/10/week-in-review-cpanel-vulnerability-actively-exploited-digicert-breach-linkedin-job-scams/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-11
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|re: '^/opt/panlogs/tmp/authd_core\..*\.gz$'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of core dump files for the 'authd' process, a potential indicator of successful or attempted exploitation of CVE-2026-0300.

relationships.source_name: Palo Alto Networks Captive Portal Access Attempt
Palo Alto Networks authd Process Crash
Palo Alto Networks authd Core Dump File Creation
